perturbation.original_text: Without granting any right or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any information after five years following the disclosure thereof or any inform ation that the Receiving Party can document (i) is or becomes (through no improper action or inaction by the Receiving Party or any affiliate, agent, consultant or employee) generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing, except to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Propr ietary Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party may make disclosures required by law or court order provided the Receiving Party uses diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure and has allowed the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.changed_text: Without granting any right or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any information after one year following the disclosure thereof or any inform ation that the Receiving Party can document (i) is or becomes (through no improper action or inaction by the Receiving Party or any affiliate, agent, consultant or employee) generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing, except to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Propr ietary Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party may make disclosures required by law or court order provided the Receiving Party uses diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure and has allowed the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states a five-year limit on the applicability of confidentiality, while the modified version reduces it to one year. This creates an in-text contradiction with the first section that states the Receiving Party will hold the Proprietary Information in strict confidence without this limitation. This creates confusion on how long the the confidentiality obligation will last.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the disclosure of Proprietary Information by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party hereby agrees: (i) to hold the Proprietary Information in st rict confidence and to take all reasonable precautions to protect such Proprietary Information (including, without limitation, all precautions the Receiving Party employs with respect to its own confidential materials), (ii) not to disclose any such Propri etary Information or any information derived therefrom to any third person, (iii) not to make any use whatsoever at any time of such Proprietary Information except to evaluate internally its relationship with the Disclosing Party, and (iv) not to copy or r everse engineer any such Proprietary Information. The Receiving Party shall procure that its employees, agents and sub - contractors to whom Proprietary Information is disclosed or who have access to Proprietary Information sign a nondisclosure or similar ag reement in content substantially similar to this Agreement
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a five-year limit on confidentiality, while the changed text reduces it to one year. The contradicted text states that the Receiving Party will hold the Proprietary Information in strict confidence without any such time limitation. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency as the time limit for confidentiality is now ambiguous. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, as different sections provide conflicting obligations regarding the duration of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may make disclosures required by law or court order provided the Receiving Party uses diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure and has allowed the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party is strictly prohibited from making any disclosures required by law or court order, regardless of any efforts to limit disclosure or allowing the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement allows for disclosures required by law or court order, subject to reasonable efforts to protect confidentiality. The modified agreement completely prohibits such disclosures. This is contradictory if a court orders the disclosure of 'Proprietary Information' but the contract forbids it. This contradicts section 1. as well. Where the receiving party should hold the Proprietary Information in strict confidence, which could be interpreted as it has the autonomy to disclose in certain conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the disclosure of Proprietary Information by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party hereby agrees: (i) to hold the Proprietary Information in st rict confidence and to take all reasonable precautions to protect such Proprietary Information (including, without limitation, all precautions the Receiving Party employs with respect to its own confidential materials), (ii) not to disclose any such Propri etary Information or any information derived therefrom to any third person, (iii) not to make any use whatsoever at any time of such Proprietary Information except to evaluate internally its relationship with the Disclosing Party, and (iv) not to copy or r everse engineer any such Proprietary Information. The Receiving Party shall procure that its employees, agents and sub - contractors to whom Proprietary Information is disclosed or who have access to Proprietary Information sign a nondisclosure or similar ag reement in content substantially similar to this Agreement
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text allows for disclosures required by law or court order if reasonable efforts are made to limit disclosure, while the changed text strictly prohibits such disclosures, 'regardless of any efforts to limit disclosure'. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which requires the Receiving Party to hold Proprietary Information in strict confidence, but which could be reasonably interpreted to allow disclosure in certain circumstances. The modified text removes any such interpretation, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representation or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended at any time by the Disclosing Party unilaterally, with or without prior notice to the Receiving Party. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representation or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires written agreement from both parties to amend the agreement. The modified version allows the Disclosing Party to amend unilaterally. This creates a contradiction as the rest of the agreement implies mutual agreement and obligation, whereas now one party holds amendment power, undermining the previous statement of understanding between the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall not b e construed as a waiver of such rights. If any part, term or provision of this Agreement is held to be illegal or unenforceable neither the validity, nor enforceability of the remainder of this Agreement shall be affected. Neither Party shall assign or tra nsfer all or any part of its rights under this Agreement without the consent of the other Party. This Agreement may not be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representation or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text both state that the agreement may not be amended without the prior written agreement of both parties. The changed text allows the Disclosing Party to unilaterally amend the agreement, directly contradicting the original agreement, and the contradicted text, which necessitates mutual agreement for amendments. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document regarding the amendment process.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the jurisdiction in which the Disclosing Party is located (or if the Disclosing Party is based in more than one country, the country in which its headquart ers are located) (the "Territory") and the parties agree to submit disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement to the non -exclusive of the courts in the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the jurisdiction in which the Receiving Party is located (or if the Receiving Party is based in more than one country, the country in which its headquart ers are located) (the "Territory") and the parties agree to submit disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement to the non -exclusive of the courts in the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that the governing law is that of the Disclosing Party's jurisdiction, while the modified version switches it to the Receiving Party's jurisdiction. This can lead to legal uncertainty and increased costs, and creates internal contradiction because the disclosing party is the one sharing the proprietary information, implying that it should be protected under its own jurisdiction laws.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party understands that the Disclosing Party has disclosed or may disclose information relating to the meeting of the SDSS -III collaboration. Proprietary data and papers in preparation based on those data will be discussed. Comments and participation in discussions during this meeting do not imply any expectation or reciprocation of co -authorship on published works. To the extent previously, presently, or subsequently disclosed to the Receiving Party is hereinafte r referred to as "Proprietary Information" of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the governing law is that of the Disclosing Party, which aligns with the Contradicted Text, as the Disclosing Party's proprietary information is being discussed. The changed text switches the governing law to the Receiving Party's jurisdiction. This creates a direct conflict because the Disclosing Party's proprietary information, as described in the Contradicted Text, would then be subject to the Receiving Party's laws, potentially undermining the Disclosing Party's legal protections and control over its own Proprietary Information, thus resulting in a strong in-text contradiction.
